name: Suspicious Get-Variable Creation
notes:
This rule detects PowerShell Persistence via hijacking Get-Variable.exe.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: frack113
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Get-Variable.exe Creation
SIGMA Description: Get-Variable is a valid PowerShell cmdlet
    WindowsApps is by default in the path where PowerShell is executed.
    So when the Get-Variable command is issued on PowerShell execution, the system first looks for the Get-Variable executable in the path and executes the malicious binary instead of looking for the PowerShell cmdlet.


Confidence: MEDIUM

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event QID is one of the following (5001830) FileCreate
when the event matches Target File Directory (custom) contains any of Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\Get-Variable.exe
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Get-Variable Creation on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event QID is one of the following (5001830) FileCreate
AND when the event matches Target File Directory (custom) contains any of Local\Microsoft\WindowsApps\Get-Variable.exe
RED_list: Event description: Detects PowerShell Persistence via hijacking Get-Variable.exe.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
